title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. A traditional Middle Eastern dish perfect for special occasions. Get step-by-step instructions!
longTailKeywords: authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
h2Headings: What is Lamb Ouzi? A Traditional Middle Eastern Dish
Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
How to Make Lamb Ouzi: Step-by-Step Guide
Expert Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Serving Suggestions & Pairings for Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi dish, garnished and ready to serve, showcasing the tender lamb and flavorful rice.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of this traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow roasted to perfection for an unforgettable meal.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish featuring tender, slow-roasted lamb served over a fragrant spiced rice pilaf. This recipe captures the essence of a celebratory meal, with meat that falls off the bone and aromatic rice bursting with flavor. Prepare to impress your guests with this authentic and heartwarming culinary masterpiece.

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of Seven Spice blend, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. Rub this spice mixture generously all over the lamb. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil. Roast the lamb for 4 to 4.5 hours, or until it is fork-tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste occasionally with its own juices if desired. Remove the foil for the last 30 minutes to allow the lamb to brown slightly. Once done, remove from oven, cover loosely with foil, and let it rest while you prepare the rice.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics for Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb rests, drain the soaked Basmati rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Season and Cook the Rice
instructions.text: Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of Seven Spice blend, cinnamon powder, cardamom powder, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the drained Basmati rice and stir gently to coat the grains with the spices. Pour in the broth and add the bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.

instructions.title: Finish the Rice Pilaf
instructions.text: Once the rice is cooked, remove it from the heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff the rice gently with a fork. Stir in the frozen green peas until combined. The residual heat will cook the peas.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve Lamb Ouzi
instructions.text: To serve, spread the fragrant spiced rice pilaf onto a large serving platter. Carefully place the whole slow-roasted lamb on top of the rice. Garnish generously with toasted pine nuts, blanched almonds, and fresh chopped parsley or mint. Serve immediately and enjoy this magnificent Middle Eastern feast.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, marinate the lamb overnight in the spice mixture before roasting. This allows the spices to penetrate deeper into the meat.
Always toast your nuts (pine nuts, almonds) separately in a dry pan or oven until golden. This enhances their flavor and adds a delightful crunch to the final dish.
To ensure perfectly fluffy rice, avoid lifting the lid while it simmers. Once cooked, let it rest for a few minutes before fluffing to allow the steam to redistribute evenly.
article:
Experience the rich tapestry of Middle Eastern culinary tradition with a dish that embodies hospitality, flavor, and festive spirit: Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent creation, featuring whole slow-roasted lamb infused with aromatic spices and often served atop a bed of fragrant rice, is more than just a meal; it's a celebration. Perfect for special occasions, family gatherings, or simply when you desire a truly impressive centerpiece, a well-executed Lamb Ouzi promises an unforgettable dining experience. If you're searching for an authentic Lamb Ouzi recipe that will transport your taste buds to the heart of the Levant, you've come to the right place. We'll guide you through the art of slow roasting to achieve fall-off-the-bone tenderness and unparalleled flavor.

## What is Lamb Ouzi? A Traditional Middle Eastern Dish

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzy" or "Ozi," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish renowned for its grandeur and deliciousness. At its core, it features succulent lamb, traditionally a whole lamb or a significant portion like a leg or shoulder, slow-roasted until it’s incredibly tender and flavorful. This exquisite meat is typically served over a bed of spiced basmati rice, often studded with toasted nuts like pine nuts, almonds, and pistachios, and sometimes peas or raisins. The true magic of a great Lamb Ouzi recipe lies in the meticulous preparation, particularly the long marination and slow cooking process that allows the lamb to absorb a complex array of spices, resulting in a deeply aromatic and meltingly tender dish. It's a dish synonymous with generosity and celebration, frequently gracing tables during Eid, weddings, and large family feasts across countries like Jordan, Syria, Lebanon, and Palestine.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi recipe requires a selection of quality ingredients that work in harmony to build its signature flavor profile. Gathering the right components is the first step to mastering your Lamb Ouzi recipe.

**For the Lamb (approx. 4-6 kg lamb shoulder or leg, bone-in):**
*   1 whole lamb shoulder or leg, bone-in (approx. 4-6 kg)
*   1/2 cup olive oil
*   1/4 cup lemon juice
*   1 head garlic, cloves minced
*   2 large onions, roughly chopped
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt (optional, but helps tenderize)
*   **Spice Blend:**
    *   2 tbsp ground allspice
    *   1 tbsp ground cardamom
    *   1 tbsp ground cinnamon
    *   1 tbsp ground cumin
    *   1 tbsp black pepper
    *   1 tsp ground nutmeg
    *   1 tsp turmeric (for color)
    *   2 tbsp salt (or to taste)

**For the Spiced Rice:**
*   4 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   6 cups lamb or chicken stock
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/4 cup olive oil or ghee
*   **Spice Blend:**
    *   1 tbsp ground allspice
    *   1 tsp ground cinnamon
    *   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
    *   1/2 tsp black pepper
    *   1 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   1 cup frozen peas (optional)
*   1/2 cup toasted pine nuts
*   1/2 cup toasted slivered almonds
*   Fresh parsley or cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## How to Make Lamb Ouzi: Step-by-Step Guide

Following this detailed Lamb Ouzi recipe will ensure a spectacular outcome, producing a dish that is both a feast for the eyes and the palate.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the surface of the lamb deeply in a diamond pattern, about 1 inch apart, ensuring the cuts penetrate about an inch into the meat. This allows the marinade to deeply infuse the lamb.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, chopped onions, yogurt (if using), and all the lamb spice blend ingredients (allspice, cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, black pepper, nutmeg, turmeric, salt). Mix well to form a thick paste.
3.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Rub the marinade generously all over the lamb, making sure to work it into the scored cuts. Place the marinated lamb in a large roasting pan, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 12 hours, or preferably 24-48 hours, for maximum flavor penetration and tenderness.
4.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 150°C (300°F). Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for about an hour. Add 1-2 cups of water or stock to the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the pan tightly with foil, ensuring no steam can escape. Roast for 4-5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and practically falling off the bone. The cooking time will vary depending on the size of your lamb cut.
5.  **Brown the Lamb:** Once tender, remove the foil. Increase the oven temperature to 200°C (400°F). Roast for another 30-45 minutes, or until the lamb skin is beautifully browned and slightly crispy. Baste occasionally with the pan juices.
6.  **Rest the Lamb:** Remove the lamb from the oven and transfer it to a large carving board. Tent loosely with foil and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This is a crucial step that allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a moist and tender result.
7.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb is resting, prepare the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil or ghee over medium heat. Sauté the finely chopped onion until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the rice spice blend (allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, salt) and cook for 30 seconds until aromatic. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat evenly with the spices. Pour in the lamb or chicken stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. If using, stir in the frozen peas during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
8.  **Assemble and Serve:** Fluff the rice with a fork and spread it onto a large serving platter. Carefully carve the slow-roasted lamb, or simply pull it apart with forks, and arrange it over the spiced rice. Garnish generously with toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley or cilantro.

## Expert Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Achieving an unforgettable Lamb Ouzi requires attention to a few key details. These insights will elevate your Lamb Ouzi recipe from good to unforgettable.

*   **Don't Skimp on Marination Time:** The longer the lamb marinates, the more flavor it will absorb, and the more tender it will become. Overnight is good, but 24-48 hours is truly ideal for a whole lamb or large cut.
*   **Low and Slow is the Key:** Patience is a virtue when roasting lamb for Ouzi. Cooking at a low temperature for an extended period breaks down tough connective tissues, resulting in meat that literally melts in your mouth.
*   **Use a Meat Thermometer:** For absolute precision, ensure the internal temperature of the lamb reaches 63°C (145°F) for medium-rare, or up to 71°C (160°F) for medium. For fall-apart tender Ouzi, you're aiming for closer to 85-90°C (185-195°F) when it's ready to be pulled.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** After roasting, always allow the lamb to rest. This allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, ensuring a supremely moist and flavorful result. Cutting it too soon will cause the juices to run out, leaving you with dry meat.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Don't skip toasting the pine nuts and almonds. This simple step brings out their nutty flavor and adds a delightful crunch, elevating the texture of the dish.
*   **Quality Stock for Rice:** Using good quality lamb or chicken stock for the rice significantly enhances its flavor compared to just water.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a showstopper on its own, but it truly shines when complemented by traditional Middle Eastern side dishes. No matter the occasion, a well-executed Lamb Ouzi recipe is sure to impress.

*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple bowl of plain yogurt, perhaps mixed with a little crushed garlic and dried mint, provides a refreshing contrast to the rich lamb. Tzatziki or labneh can also work wonderfully.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Light, vibrant salads cut through the richness. Think Fattoush (a bread salad with mixed greens, vegetables, and toasted pita) or Tabbouleh (a finely chopped parsley and bulgur salad).
*   **Pickles:** Assorted pickled vegetables (turnips, cucumbers, chilies) offer a tangy counterpoint.
*   **Warm Bread:** Freshly baked pita bread is perfect for scooping up the lamb and rice.
*   **Drinks:** For a traditional pairing, consider Arak (an anise-flavored alcoholic drink diluted with water), or simply enjoy with sparkling water with mint and lemon, or a robust red wine.

## Lamb Ouzi Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Lamb Ouzi recipe is magnificent, don't hesitate to explore variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Different Meats:** While lamb is traditional, this slow-roasting method can be adapted for goat or even a large cut of beef (like a chuck roast or short ribs) for a similar tender result, though the flavor profile will change.
*   **Stuffed Ouzi:** Some ambitious versions involve deboning the lamb shoulder or leg, stuffing it with the spiced rice mixture, then rolling and roasting it. This creates an impressive presentation and ensures the rice absorbs even more lamb flavor.
*   **Rice Add-ins:** Feel free to customize the rice. Add chickpeas, golden raisins, or finely diced carrots for extra texture and sweetness.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Adjust the spice blend to your preference. If you love a stronger cardamom note, add more. If you prefer less cinnamon, reduce it. A touch of dried rose petals can add a subtle, elegant fragrance to the rice.
*   **Simplified Ouzi:** For a quicker weeknight version, you can use lamb shanks or smaller lamb chops, marinate them for a shorter period, and then braise them until tender, serving them over the same spiced rice. It won't be "whole slow roasted," but it captures the essence of the flavor.

Creating Lamb Ouzi is a culinary journey that rewards patience and passion with a dish that is truly extraordinary. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and delight in the reactions of your guests as they experience this iconic Middle Eastern feast.
